content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
R. W. Goff 
San Jose/Evergreen Community 
College District 
4750 San Felipe Road 
San Jose, CA 
95135-1599 
Dear Mr. Goff: 
February 14, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-032 
We have received your request for advice regarding the mass 
mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") .1/ 
As 
the Chancellor of the San Jose/Evergreen Community College 
District, you want to ensure that the schedule of classes, which 
is mailed to every household in the district, is in compliance 
with the new mass mailing provisions contained in Proposition 73. 
QUESTION 
May the names of the elected governing board of trustees be 
printed in the Community College District schedule of classes? 
CONCLUSION 
The roster listing exception to the mass mailing provisions 
of the Act permits the schedule of classes to list the names of 
the chancellor, college presidents and board of trustees, provided 
that their names appear in the same typeface, size and location in 
the layout. 
FACTS 
The San Jose/Evergreen Community College District is governed 
by an elected ~~ard of trustees, which represents approximately 
800,000 people. 
Everything that is done in the community college 
district is subject to the control of the board of trustees. 
As 
1/ 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
Section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to 
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


R. W. Goff 
February 14, 1989 
Page 2 
specifically regards the schedule of classes, the board authorizes 
the money for developing content, printing and mailing. 
The 
schedule of classes is mailed to every household in the district. 
Your request states that you would like to include the fol-
lowing sentence on a page of the schedule of classes: 
"If you 
want to communicate with any of the officers of the San Jose/ 
Evergreen Community College District, the following is offered for 
your convenience." 
Beneath this will appear the name of the 
chancellor, college presidents, and the heading "Board of 
Trustees," followed by the names of the elected trustees. 
The 
trustees' names will be in the same type size as the other names. 
ANALYSIS 
section 89001 provides that no newsletter or other mass mail-
ing shall be sent at public expense. 
At its December 6, 1988 
meeting, the Commission adopted Regulation 18901 (copy 
enclosed) ,2/ which implements Section 89001. 
Regulation 18901(a) 
states as follows: 
(a) 
Except as provided herein, a newsletter 
or other mass mailing is "sent at public expense" 
within the meaning of Government Code Section 89001 
if any of the costs of design, production, printing 
or distribution is paid for with public moneys as 
defined in Government Code section 85102(e). 
Regulation 18901(e) establishes an exception to the mass 
mailing prohibitions if certain criteria are met. 
Regulation 
18901(e) provides: 
(e) 
A newsletter or other mass mailing is not 
prohibited by Government Code section 89001 if it 
meets all of the following criteria: 
(1) The stationery, forms and envelopes 
used for the mailing are the standard 
stationery, forms and envelopes of the agency 
or committee of the agency; and 
(2) The name of an elected officer who is 
affiliated with the agency or :Jmmittee ap-
pears, in the standard letternead or logotype 
of the stationery, forms or envelopes of the 
agency, a committee of the agency, or the 
2/ 
This regulation is under review with the Office of 
Administrative Law. 
We advise that the regulation be followed 
while it is undergoing review and we anticipate that it will be in 
effect on approximately April I, 1989. 


R. W. Goff 
February 14, 1989 
Page 3 
elected official and the newsletter or mass 
mailing is not otherwise prohibited under 
subdivision (c). 
As used in this subdivision, the term "let-
terhead or logotype" includes a listing of agency 
or committee officials or members, in which all who 
are listed appear in the same typeface and type 
size and location in the layout of the newsletter 
or other mass mailing. 
As used in this SUbdivision, the term 
"standard letterhead or logotype" refers to any 
regularly used by the agency, subunit or the 
elected officer. 
Provided, however, that where a 
newsletter does not use the agency's standard 
stationery letterhead, a roster listing containing 
the names of all elected officers in the agency may 
be used in the newsletter in place of the agency's 
standard stationery letterhead. 
(Emphasis added.) 
This roster listing exception applies to your inquiry, 
provided that all of the names listed appear in the same typeface, 
size and location in the layout of the class schedule and that no 
other mention is made of the officials. 
I hope this letter answers your question regarding the mass 
mailing requirements as they pertain to class schedules. 
If you 
have any questions, please call me at (916) 322-5901. 
DMG:JRS:ld 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
By: 
v,~,~~' 
~ 
techer 
el, Legal Division 


San Jose/Evergreen Community College District JAN 17 
January 13, 1989 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Legal Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Attention: John McLean 
Dear Mr. McLean: 
FPPC 
3 ~3 fi! '09 
4750 San Felipe Road 
San Jose. California 95135-1599 
(408) 274-6700 
R. W. Goff. Chancellor 
San Jose City College 
Evergreen Valley College 
Much discussion has taken place with regard to what Proposition 73 
does and does not prohibit. What I need is a rather simple yes 
or no as to whether I can print the names of the Governing Board 
(they are elected) in our schedule of classes which is mailed to 
every household in our district. 
Our District Board of Trustees represents a population of approxi-
mately 800,000 people. The trustees are essentially low visibility 
because the newspaper and other media do little or no coverage of 
our institution. If a person receives a schedule of classes mailed 
to his home and he wants to write a letter of concern to our 
trustees, he does not know the names of our trustees in order to 
address the letter. It is also probable that he does not know 
that we are governed by a board of trustees, so he really doesn't 
know how to address the letter in order to get to the top decision 
makers. 
The FPPC regulations state that the name of an officer can not 
be listed in a mass mailing if the elected officer exercises 
direction or control over the content, production, or distri-
bution of the document. This remains vague in terms of our 
mailing of the schedule of classes. Everything that is done 
in our community college district is subject to the control 
of our Board of Trustees. -With regard to our schedule of 
classes the Board authorizes us to expend the money for 
developing content, printing and mailing. So to that extent 
they control and direct. Does that preclude them from having 
their names in the schedule? 
" 
Governing Board-Charles R. Blackmore • Richard A. Bowers • Robert M. Giuli • Mary Ruth Gross • Charlotte Powers 


Fair Political Practices Commission 
January 13, 1989 
What I would like to do is state on a page in the schedule of classes, 
II If you want to conununicate with any of the offi cers of the San Jose/ 
Evergreen Community College District, the following is offered for 
your convenience." Then name the chancellor and college presidents 
and under the heading "Board of Trustees" printTn same type-size 
the names of the elected trustees. 
May I do that? 
Sincerely, 
!lrdtt+ 
R. W. Goff 
Chancellor 
RWG:mp 
cc: Trustee Richard Bowers 
-2-


San Jose/Evergreen Community College District J 
! 7 
aN I. 
January 13, 1989 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Legal Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Attention: John McLean 
Dear Mr. McLean: 
I::" tJ ~; :" 
: 
i. 
3 ~3 hi f89 
4750 San Felipe Road 
San Jose, California 95135-1599 
(408) 274-6700 
R. W. Goff. Chancellor 
San Jose City College 
Evergreen Valley College 
Much discussion has taken place with regard to what PropOSition 73 
does and does not prohibit. 
What I need is a rather simple yes 
or no as to whether I can print the names of the Governing Board 
(they are elected) in our schedule of classes which is mailed to 
every household in our district. 
Our District Board of Trustees represents a population of approxi-
mately 800,000 people. The trustees are essentially low visibility 
because the newspaper and other media do little or no coverage of 
our institution. If a person receives a schedule of classes mailed 
to his home and he wants to write a letter of concern to our 
trustees, he does not know the names of our trustees in order to 
address the letter. It is also probable that he does not know 
that we are governed by a board of trustees, so he really doesn't 
know how to address the letter in order to get to the top decision 
makers. 
The FPPC regulations state that the name of an officer can not 
be listed in a mass mailing if the elected officer exercises 
direction or control over the content, production, or distri-
bution of the document. 
This remains vague in terms of our 
mailing of the schedule of classes. Everything that is done 
in our community college district is subject to the control 
of our Board of Trustees. 
With regard to our schedule of 
classes the Board authorizes us to expend the money for 
developing content, printing and mailing. So to that extent 
they control and direct. 
Does that preclude them from having 
their names in the schedule? 
Governing Board-Charles R. Blackmore • Richard A. Bowers • Robert M. Giuli • Mary Ruth Gross· Charlotte Powers 


Fair Political Practices Commission 
January 13, 1989 
What I would like to do is state on a page in the schedule of classes, 
IIIf you want to communicate with any of the officers of the San Jose/ 
Evergreen Community College District, the following is offered for 
your convenience. II 
Then name the chancellor and college presidents 
and under the heading "Board of Trustees" print in same type size 
the names of the elected trustees. 
May I do that? 
Sincerely, 
!ltd, It+ 
R. W. Goff 
Chancellor

Trustee Richard Bowers

QUESTION: May the names of the elected governing board of trustees be 
printed in the Community College District schedule of classes?

CONCLUSION: The roster listing exception to the mass mailing provisions 
of the Act permits the schedule of classes to list the names of 
the chancellor, college presidents and board of trustees, provided 
that their names appear in the same typeface, size and location in 
the layout.
